_comment: Auto-generated by src/lib/_demos/generateNodeManifest.svelte.test.js (GEN_MANIFEST=1). Do not hand-edit. Consumed by the Chronobiology Handbook Node Reference page.

nodes.description: Actogram (double-plotted activity over days).
Boxplot summarising the distribution of a column by group.

Circular phase plot: one or more phase columns on an adjustable-period clock, each a coloured group with its Rayleigh mean-resultant vector; optional rose wedges and a Watson-Williams equal-mean-direction test. Wiring an optional time column (hours or timestamp, folded by period) to a series plots it at its measurement time with radius = value instead, and the vector becomes an amplitude-weighted acrophase.
Coloured correlation matrix computed from the wired columns.
Autocorrelation / cross-correlation plot.
Inspector for a plot's computed data (bins, spectra, fitted values); opened from a plot's View data action.
Fourier analysis (FFT) spectrum of a time-series.
Histogram of a column.
Mean ± SEM overlay: per-group mean marker with standard-error whiskers.
Scatterplot matrix (pairs.panels): histograms on the diagonal, scatter + fit above, correlation below.
Periodogram (Lomb-Scargle / chi-squared) of a time-series.
Scatterplot of one column versus another.
Tabular display of a table or its summary statistics.
Add a constant or column to the input column.
Edit or substitute individual values in a column.
Filter rows based on the values of another column.
FFT-based low-, high-, or band-pass filter over an evenly sampled series.
Multiply the input column by a constant or another column.
Normalize the column to a chosen range or by a reference value.
Detect and remove outliers from the column.
Remove a fitted trend (linear, polynomial, etc.) from the column.
Find a specific value in the column and replace it with another (e.g. swap a missing-data code like −999 for a different value).
Average daily profile — fold a series onto one period and show the per-bin mean (± SEM).
Bin time-series values into regular intervals and aggregate per bin.
Enter data by hand, or paste CSV/JSON.
Chi-squared test: independence of two categorical variables (contingency table), or goodness-of-fit of counts against a uniform expectation.
Circadian Function Index (0–1) summarising rest-activity rhythm robustness from nonparametric IS, IV and RA (Ortiz-Tudela et al. 2010).
Collect (concatenate) several columns into one.
Apply a per-column aggregate function (mean, sum, etc.) across rows or groups.
Curate a live subset of columns by name/label and reuse it as one wire.
Pairwise correlation matrix (Pearson or Spearman) across the wired columns, with normality-based auto method choice.
Fit a cosinor model (period, amplitude, acrophase) to the column.
Cross-correlogram of two series: the correlation at each lag, with the peak lag revealing the delay between them.
Per-column summary statistics (mean, median, sd, min/max, quartiles, skewness, kurtosis).
Fit a double-logistic curve to the column (useful for phenology / on-off transitions).
Fit an arbitrary user-supplied function to the column.
Compute a new column from a user-supplied formula referencing other columns.
Compare statistics between groups defined by another column.
Fill missing values or resample a series onto a regular grid by interpolation (linear, nearest, or cubic spline).
Model a binary outcome from one or more predictors: coefficients, odds ratios with 95% CIs, Wald p-values, and model fit (pseudo-R², LR test).
Pivot a long-format table into wide format.
Compute a rolling-window analysis across the column.
Nonparametric rest-activity variables (IS, IV, RA, M10/L5) — robust to non-sinusoidal rhythms.
Test each wired column for normality (D’Agostino-Pearson or Jarque-Bera), reporting the statistic, p-value and a verdict.
Generate a column of random values.

Circular statistics on phase/angle columns: the Rayleigh uniformity test (R, z, p, acrophase) per column, with an optional Watson-Williams equal-mean-direction test (F, p) across columns. Wiring an optional time column (hours or timestamp, folded by period) switches to an amplitude-weighted mean vector/acrophase; without it, values are treated as raw angles.
Fit a rectangular (square) wave model with adjustable duty cycle.
Run rhythmicity statistics over the column.
Create a numeric or time sequence column.
Generate a synthetic time-series table from a cosine + noise model.
Smooth a column using LOESS, moving average, Savitzky-Golay, or Whittaker-Eilers.
Sort rows by a chosen column, keeping the selected columns aligned (ascending or descending).
Split a column or table on a delimiter or group key.
Group derived stored values together for reuse.
Fit and store a trend (linear, polynomial, exponential, logarithmic) over the column.
Pivot a wide-format table into long format.
